case_name: It is in addition only in comparatively rare cases that this court interferes with concurrent findings of fact by lower courts. As Lord Jauncey of Tullichettle explained inHiggins v J & C M Smith (Whiteinch) Ltd1990 SC (HL) 63, 82:

summary.brief: It is in addition only in comparatively rare cases that this court interferes with concurrent findings of fact by lower courts. As Lord Jauncey of Tullichettle explained inHiggins v J & C M Smith (Whiteinch) Ltd1990 SC (HL) 63, 82:. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Nadine Montgomery gave birth to a baby boy on 1 October 1999 at Bellshill Maternity Hospital, Lanarkshire. As a result of complications during the delivery, the baby was born with severe disabilities.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The first concerned her ante-natal care. It was contended that she ought to have been given advice about the risk of shoulder dystocia... and of the alternative possibility of delivery by elective caesarean section.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: During the 12 minutes between the baby's head appearing and the delivery, the umbilical cord was completely or partially occluded, depriving him of oxygen.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Philip Owen, an expert witness who gave evidence on behalf of the Board, as 'a major obstetric emergency associated with a short and long term neonatal and maternal morbidity [and] an associated neonatal mortality'.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Philip Owen

evidence_index.text: The Lord Ordinary, Lord Bannatyne, rejected both grounds of fault: [2010] CSOH 104.
evidence_index.speaker: Lord Bannatyne

evidence_index.text: The court has been invited to depart from the decision of the House of Lords in Sidaway and to re-consider the duty of a doctor towards a patient in relation to advice about treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Mrs Montgomery was told that she was having a larger than usual baby. But she was not told about the risks of her experiencing mechanical problems during labour.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr McLellan accepted that this was a high risk. But, despite the risk, she said that her practice was not to spend a lot of time, or indeed any time at all, discussing potential risks of shoulder dystocia.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. McLellan

evidence_index.text: For these reasons, we would allow the appeal.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: The question should properly have been addressed as to Mrs Montgomery's likely reaction if she had been told of the risk of shoulder dystocia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mrs. Montgomery was expecting her first baby and was under the care of Dr. McLellan at Bellshill Maternity Hospital. She was a diabetic patient, which made her pregnancy high risk.
critical_findings: High risk of shoulder dystocia due to diabetes

requestables.name: Obstetric Consultation
requestables.description: Discussion of delivery options and associated risks

reveal.result_summary: Risk of shoulder dystocia not disclosed
reveal.clinical_significance: Patient not informed of significant risk associated with vaginal delivery
reveal.decision_impact: Lack of informed consent for delivery method

timeline_phases.name: Initial Obstetric Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Discussion of delivery options for diabetic patient

key_events.event: Patient informed of larger than usual baby

decision_points.clinical_context: Pregnant patient with known risk factors
decision_points.prompt: What should be discussed with the patient regarding delivery options?

options.description: Discuss risk of shoulder dystocia and offer elective caesarean section
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient should be informed of all material risks to make an informed decision

options.description: Proceed with vaginal delivery without discussing risks
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant withheld information to avoid patient opting for caesarean section

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not disclose risk of shoulder dystocia
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed disclosure would lead to unnecessary caesarean requests

expected_action_court.description: Risk of shoulder dystocia should have been disclosed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Patient autonomy and informed consent principles

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to disclose material risk violated informed consent
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been informed of all risks and alternatives

patient_outcome.description: Baby suffered from shoulder dystocia during delivery

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to disclose risk of shoulder dystocia
Causation established - informed consent not obtained

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to disclose risk of shoulder dystocia
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Disclosure would have led to elective caesarean section

factual_timeline.event: Nadine Montgomery gave birth at Bellshill Maternity Hospital

allegations.allegation: Failure to disclose risk of shoulder dystocia

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by not disclosing risk
